title: Before the Gates Open
desc: The Colosseum is at its strangest before the crowd arrives, when the arena has to be made ready for spectacle.

steps.text:
The UnderWorld Colosseum is loud by reputation and silent by dawn. Before the crowd arrives, before the betting slips start changing claws and before the first fighter decides to pace holes into the holding floor, the arena belongs to work crews and the low red light of waking braziers. Empty galleries curve up into shadow. The central pit lies smooth and waiting below, red sand dark where last night's wash water still clings at the edges. Even without an audience, the place feels like it expects blood.

The pit master hands you a rake with iron teeth on one side and a flat smoothing blade on the other. "Upper braziers if you want the broad view," he says. "Pit floor if you want to stand where the shouting will happen later. Either job has to be right before the gates open."

options.label: Climb the gallery line and set the braziers burning true
Rake and smooth the arena floor before the first bout

steps.text:
The braziers run in a long crescent above the seating tiers, great iron bowls that fill the galleries with heat, smoke, and the kind of light that flatters violence. A few are already burning, but two sit low and another is throwing more smoke than flame because yesterday's clinker was left too long in the bowl. You clear the worst of it, reset the fuel bed, and coax the line back into an even red blaze that turns the old stone walls gold and black by turns.

From up here the arena opens below you like the inside of some patient furnace. The pit looks smaller from the gallery and more dangerous for it. The seats, still empty, already seem crowded in promise. When the last brazier catches, the whole Colosseum changes from ruin to theater. The pit master glances up at the completed line and grunts approval. "Good. Now it looks like something worth surviving."

options.label: Climb down and leave the galleries burning bright

steps.text:
The arena floor is never just sand. It is raked, graded, watered, and re-raked until it gives fighters footing without hiding loose stone or yesterday's damage beneath a shallow lie. You draw the iron teeth through the surface in long even passes, break up the clumped sections near the center, and smooth the outer ring where yesterday's rush to clean left shallow ridges.

It is repetitive work in a place built for drama, which may be why it matters so much. By the time you finish, the whole pit reads cleanly again under the red light from above: no soft pockets, no slick patches, no raised strip waiting to twist an ankle at the wrong time. The pit master drops a weighted dummy shield in the center, watches how it settles, and nods once. "Good. Let the fighters blame each other. Not the floor."

options.label: Lean the rake aside and leave the pit ready for spectacle
